name: Vampire: The Masquerade — Modern Nights
description: World of Darkness / V5 lorebook covering Kindred society, the 13 clans, sects, disciplines, and the metaplot of the modern nights. Designed for AI roleplay sessions in V:tM settings.

0.content: The World of Darkness is our world, but worse — gothic-punk, oppressive, where shadows are deeper and corruption runs through every institution. Vampires (called Kindred) have walked alongside humanity for millennia, hidden behind a code of secrecy called the Masquerade. The current era is the modern nights — the late 2020s, the Information Age made hostile to monsters who require darkness. Smartphones in every pocket, surveillance-state cameras, biometric tracking, and the Second Inquisition (a shadow alliance of mortal intelligence agencies) hunting Kindred with unprecedented efficiency. Most cities of any size have a Kindred population — sometimes ruled by a Camarilla Prince, sometimes claimed by Anarch Barons, sometimes contested. The Beckoning, a metaphysical compulsion drawing ancient vampires to the Middle East, has emptied the world of elders, leaving younger generations to inherit a fracturing system. This is a setting of personal horror, bad faith, monstrous appetite, and the slow grinding loss of one's humanity.

1.content: The First Tradition: Reveal not your true nature to those not of the Blood; doing so renders you forfeit to the wrath of your elders. The Masquerade is not just a law — it is the load-bearing beam of Kindred existence. Every vampire, regardless of sect, understands that exposure means a global pogrom they cannot survive. In the modern nights, the Masquerade is harder to maintain than ever: every mortal carries a recording device, every transaction creates a digital trail, every suspicious death lights up federal databases. A Masquerade Breach can range from minor (witnesses who can be Dominated to forget) to catastrophic (viral video, federal investigation). Maintaining the Masquerade involves vigilance, paranoia, mind-wiping witnesses, blood-bonding key mortals, owning media outlets, and — when nothing else works — killing. The modern Camarilla treats Masquerade Breaches with extreme prejudice. The Second Inquisition feeds on every breach. Even Anarchs, who reject Camarilla authority, hold the Masquerade as practical necessity. Every scene must consider Masquerade implications. A vampire who drops fang in public has just made the night much more interesting for everyone in their domain — and not in a good way.

2.content: The Beast is the predator within — not a metaphor but a literal supernatural entity riding inside every Kindred. It hungers, it rages, it remembers no laws but the chase. The Hunger is the Beast's primary expression: a constant gnawing pressure for mortal blood that rises with each scene, each act of exertion, each moment of Discipline use. In V5 terms, the Hunger is tracked from 0 (sated) to 5 (ravenous). At Hunger 5, the Beast takes the wheel — vampires lose control, frenzy, hurt those they love, expose the Masquerade. Most Kindred feed nightly to keep Hunger manageable. Frenzy occurs when the Beast overwhelms the rider: HUNGER FRENZY (need to feed at all costs, attacking the nearest blood source), FURY FRENZY (rage, violence directed at the source of provocation), TERROR FRENZY (abject flight from fire, sunlight, true faith). A frenzied Kindred is no longer themselves. The Beast remembers no Masquerade, no Touchstones, no Convictions — only appetite. The longest-lived vampires are those who have made peace with their Beast through compromise; the most damned are those who have surrendered entirely (Wassail — permanent dominance of the Beast). The Beast is the central horror of unlife: you are a thing that wants to hurt people, and resisting it costs you something every night.

3.content: Kindred physiology: dead but animate, sustained by Vitae (vampire blood). The body does not age, does not breathe (unless willed), has no heartbeat (unless willed), runs cold to the touch. With recent feeding, Kindred can pass for living — flushed cheeks, warm skin, even sexual function. Without feeding, they appear corpse-like. Vulnerabilities are absolute. SUNLIGHT causes immediate aggravated damage; within seconds it reduces a Kindred to ash. FIRE causes aggravated damage that does not heal except with significant rest and feeding. DECAPITATION ends a Kindred permanently (Final Death). A WOODEN STAKE through the heart paralyzes (does not kill) — leaving a stationary vampire to be burned, dragged into sun, or further harmed. Kindred heal mundane injuries by spending Vitae but aggravated damage (fire, sun, claws of supernaturals, fangs of other Kindred) heals slowly. Kindred do not eat or drink mortal food; consumed food triggers immediate vomiting (Thin-Bloods are exceptions). True Faith (a mortal's genuine spiritual conviction backed by power) repels and damages Kindred. Each clan also has a clan-specific Bane: a curse from their founder that imposes weakness or compulsion. Generation matters absolutely — closer to Caine = more powerful blood, fewer constraints. A 5th-generation elder can perform feats a 13th-generation neonate cannot dream of.

4.content: The Embrace is the act of creating a new vampire: the sire drains the mortal nearly to death, then forces the mortal to drink the sire's Vitae. The mortal dies and rises within nights as Kindred. The Embrace is governed by the Second Tradition (Domain) and the Sixth (Destruction) — most sects regulate it strictly, requiring permission from the local Prince/Baron. Unsanctioned Embrace can result in execution of both sire and childe. The mortal has no say in the matter; consensual Embrace is rare and considered romantically unusual. New vampires are FLEDGLINGS (newly Embraced, often under sire's tutelage), NEONATES (Embraced within the last century), ANCILLAE (1-3 centuries), ELDERS (3-10 centuries), METHUSELAHS (millennia, mostly absent due to the Beckoning). Generation describes blood-distance from Caine, the legendary first vampire. Caine is 1st generation; the Antediluvians (clan founders, often slumbering) are 3rd generation. Most playable characters are 10th-13th generation. Lower generation = stronger Disciplines, lower Hunger pressure, capacity for greater feats. DIABLERIE (consuming another Kindred's soul along with their blood) reduces the diablerist's effective generation by one — but leaves a permanent mystical mark that Auspex can detect, and is punishable by Final Death in most domains. Diablerie is the great taboo: cannibalism made into apotheosis.

5.content: The Camarilla — the Ivory Tower — is the largest Kindred sect, founded in the 15th century to enforce the Masquerade and protect Kindred from mortal hunters. Its membership is conservative, hierarchical, and elder-dominated. Camarilla cities are ruled by Princes; below the Prince sits the Primogen Council (clan elders), enforced by the Sheriff (capital police), advised by the Seneschal (chief of staff), gossiped about by the Harpy (social arbiter), and policed against bloat by the Scourge (executes unsanctioned childer). The Camarilla nominally includes seven clans: Banu Haqim (recent defectors from Independent status), Malkavian, Nosferatu, Toreador, Tremere, Ventrue, and as of V5 the Lasombra (whose inclusion is still controversial). The Brujah and Gangrel famously seceded to the Anarch Movement in V5, gutting the Camarilla's military strength. Modern Camarilla doctrine emphasizes Masquerade enforcement, mortal infiltration, and survival under Second Inquisition pressure. The Camarilla is feudal, rule-of-elders, and outwardly civilized while concealing centuries of Jyhad scheming. New Kindred are typically presented to the Prince in Elysium and must demonstrate they can maintain the Masquerade. STATUS (the Camarilla's social currency) is gained through service, lost through breaches.

6.content: The Anarch Movement is the Camarilla's primary opposition — a loose coalition of younger Kindred, defectors, and clans who rejected elder rule. Born from medieval revolts and reignited in the 1944 Second Anarch Movement (Los Angeles), the modern Anarchs have been transformed by V5's clan defections: Brujah and Gangrel left the Camarilla en masse, joining the longtime Anarch core. Anarchs reject the Camarilla's hierarchical Princedom in favor of more democratic structures: BARONS rule territories by consent of their gangs, decisions are made through assembly, status is earned by deed rather than age. The Anarchs accept the Masquerade (pragmatic necessity) but reject the Traditions where they perpetuate elder dominance. They protect Caitiff and Thin-Bloods that the Camarilla scorns. They include their own internal factions — some near-Camarilla in conservatism, some near-Sabbat in radicalism, many a chaos of independent gangs. Anarch cities tend toward Los Angeles, Berlin, Detroit, parts of South America, working-class urban centers. The Anarchs are less coordinated than the Camarilla and more vulnerable to internal conflict, but they are also more adaptive, more willing to embrace new tactics, and far more popular with neonates. The cold war between Camarilla and Anarchs defines modern Kindred politics, with each side accusing the other of bringing the Second Inquisition's gaze.

7.content: The Sabbat — Sword of Caine — is the third major sect, though largely absent from V5's main play. Where the Camarilla treats vampirism as a curse to be hidden, the Sabbat embraces it as divine: they are Caine's chosen, monsters by sacred design, opposed to the Antediluvians (whom they believe will return to consume their descendants in Gehenna). Sabbat Kindred are typically Embraced en masse, buried alive (the 'Shovelhead Embrace'), and broken to the pack. Sabbat clans historically included Lasombra (until the V5 defection) and Tzimisce, plus many antitribu (clan defectors from Camarilla clans). The Sabbat practices VAULDERIE (a ritualized blood bond among pack members), holds Auctoritas Ritae (sect rituals), and treats Masquerade with disregard bordering on contempt — though even they recognize the practical limits of exposure. In V5, the Sabbat has largely retreated from North America and Europe, drawn by the Beckoning to wage war against Antediluvians in the Middle East. What remains in Western cities is rogue packs, splinter groups, and rumors. Most modern chronicles encounter the Sabbat as boogeymen, occasional threats, or as a faction characters' Anarch/Camarilla backgrounds remember bitterly. Sabbat-focused chronicles are rare and require explicit table buy-in given the sect's dark themes.

8.content: The Second Inquisition is the most consequential mortal threat to Kindred in centuries — a coordinated alliance of intelligence agencies (FBI Special Affairs Division, MI5, Mossad, others), Vatican operatives, and inheritor remnants of older hunter organizations who pooled their data after early-21st-century leaks revealed Kindred existence to mortal agencies. The SI operates through the TWILIGHT PROJECT (NSA-style surveillance specifically targeted at Kindred), various national equivalents, and tactical strike teams. They hunt with night-vision drones, biometric facial-recognition databases that catch century-old vampires reusing identities, satellite tracking, and military-grade flamethrowers. The SI razed Vienna's Tremere Chantry in 2008. The SI hit London hard. The SI knows about clans, generations, the Camarilla. The SI does not negotiate. Rank-and-file mostly do not know they're hunting vampires (briefings are couched as terrorism); leadership considers Kindred existential threats. Modern Camarilla doctrine on the SI: do not engage, do not provoke, hide deeper. The Second Inquisition is the cold horror that hangs over every modern chronicle: even the most powerful elder is one tracked phone call from a tactical team kicking in their haven door at noon, with thermite charges and incendiary rounds.

9.content: Clan Brujah — the Learned Clan, the Rabble, the Rebels. Once philosopher-kings of ancient Carthage, the Brujah were broken when their dream-city fell to Rome (with Ventrue help, a grudge they have not forgotten in two thousand years). Modern Brujah are passion incarnate: revolutionaries, activists, brawlers, intellectuals who lead with their fists. Their archetypes range from biker-gang anarchist to tenured radical professor to street-level antifa to hard-line union organizer. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Celerity (supernatural speed), Potence (strength), Presence (charisma). BANE: prone to Fury Frenzy on slight provocation — a Brujah's temper is a tactical liability that elders never let them forget. In V5, the Brujah seceded from the Camarilla en masse in the 21st century, joining the Anarch Movement and gutting the Tower's military arm. Many Brujah Princes simply walked out of their courts. Others stayed and now find themselves outsiders in old halls. The Brujah question is always: what is worth fighting for tonight, and what is worth burning down? They are the clan most likely to die for a cause and most likely to remember why. Modern saying: the night is long; rage is longer.

10.content: Clan Gangrel — the Outlanders, the Beasts. The most feral of the great clans, Gangrel have always been closer to the Beast than other Kindred — closer to the wilds, closer to the animal. They wandered with Roma, with First Nations, with frontier outcasts, claiming no city-domain when they could claim the road. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Animalism (command of beasts), Fortitude (resilience), Protean (shapeshifting — into wolf, bat, mist, the earth itself). BANE: each frenzy leaves an animal feature on the body — fangs that won't retract, fur, eyes that stay yellow, claws that don't recede. Generations of frenzy mark the clan's elders into half-beasts, sometimes losing the human form entirely. In V5, the Gangrel followed the Brujah out of the Camarilla. Many had been considering it for centuries — the Tower never fit a clan that disliked walls. Modern Gangrel often run with Anarch packs or independently, hold rural territories the Camarilla considers too inconvenient, fight werewolves in the wild places where the two species' territories overlap. Gangrel kindred are practical, taciturn, suspicious of soft cities. They keep their word. They count their kills. They know exactly where the city ends and the dark begins.

11.content: Clan Malkavian — the Lunatics, the Madmen, the Oracles. The Malkavians are the most cursed of the great clans: their Antediluvian Malkav broke their Embrace itself, leaving every Malkavian afflicted with some form of derangement, divine madness, prophetic compulsion, or fractured perception. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Auspex (perception, mind-reading, prophetic vision), Dominate (mental control), Obfuscate (concealment). BANE: every Malkavian carries a permanent Derangement — phobias, hallucinations, compulsions, multiple personalities — varying by individual but always present, always rising under stress. Beyond personal madness, every Malkavian is connected to the MADNESS NETWORK — a psychic communion with every other Malkavian alive, sharing dreams, glimpses, fragments of the Antediluvian's screaming consciousness. They speak in riddles, deliver prophecies that come true in unexpected ways, and unsettle other Kindred who suspect the Malkavians know something everyone else has missed. They often do. Modern Malkavians are scattered across all sects — Camarilla considers them essential information sources, Anarchs respect them as siblings, Hecata trade with them carefully. Playing a Malkavian well requires depicting madness with dignity rather than as joke — the curse is real horror, the visions are real wisdom, and both cost everything.

12.content: Clan Nosferatu — the Hidden, the Sewer Rats, the Secret-Keepers. Their curse is absolute: every Nosferatu Embrace transforms the body into something monstrous — twisted, lumpen, fanged, often boil-covered or tumor-marked, always unmistakably inhuman. The Nosferatu Bane prevents them from passing for human in any context, which means they cannot move freely in society. In response, they have made the underground theirs: sewer systems, abandoned subway lines, the literal undercities beneath every great city, networks of safehouses and secret passages no one else knows about. The clan is the great information broker of Kindred society — if it happened in your domain, the Nosferatu know about it, and they will sell that information for the right price. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Animalism, Obfuscate (often shocking levels of mastery), Potence. Their network — SCHRECKNET, an illicit Kindred internet developed in the 1990s — is the single most valuable intelligence asset in the modern nights, currently being aggressively targeted by the Second Inquisition. Modern Nosferatu are loyal to clan first, sect second; they feel kinship across Camarilla/Anarch divides because they all share the curse. They are often more intellectual, more careful, more humane than their appearance suggests — and they remember every kindness or cruelty extended to them.

13.content: Clan Toreador — the Artistes, the Divas, the Roses. The Toreador are vampires in love with beauty, obsessed with art, sensation, beauty in all its forms. They are seducers, patrons, performers, critics, museum directors, fashion designers, opera singers, social butterflies, salonnières. They populate Camarilla courts as advisors and Harpies, run cultural institutions in major cities, and remember every snub at every party for a hundred years. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Auspex, Celerity, Presence. BANE: they fall into rapt fascination at scenes of beauty — a sunset (fatal), a perfect performance, a painting that catches them — losing whole minutes or longer in transfixed paralysis. Toreador divide informally into ARTISTES (who create) and POSEURS (who consume and judge); the rivalry between them is bitter and beneath the surface of every salon. Toreador are sensualists — they often retain more of their humanity longer than other clans because beauty grounds them — but they are also prone to extraordinary cruelty when their aesthetic sense is offended. Clan motto: live every night as if it were a perfect performance. Clan reality: the audience always disappoints. A Toreador in a chronicle brings glamour, gossip, and a centuries-old grudge waiting to surface.

14.content: Clan Tremere — the Warlocks, the Pyramid, the Usurpers. The Tremere are the youngest of the great clans, formed in the 11th century when a cabal of mortal mages successfully transmuted themselves into vampires through an ill-understood ritual that involved the diablerie of the Salubri Antediluvian. The clan is structured as a rigid pyramid of obedience, governed from the Vienna Chantry until that Chantry's destruction by the Second Inquisition in 2008 — an event that fractured the clan into competing houses with no clear central authority. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Auspex, Dominate, and most importantly BLOOD SORCERY (Thaumaturgy in older editions) — the rituals and powers that come from manipulating the magical properties of vampire blood itself. Tremere can bind blood, scry through it, weaponize it, store it, draw circles of warding from it. BANE: their blood is corrupted. Three drinks from a Tremere creates a Blood Bond not on the Tremere but on the drinker — a one-way devotion most clans find unsettling. Modern Tremere are scattered, adaptive, often bitter at the loss of Vienna; they are also more politically valuable than ever, as their rituals provide some of the only effective counter-measures against the Second Inquisition's tactics. They are no one's friends — but they are useful, and they remember debts.

15.content: Clan Ventrue — the Blue Bloods, the Kings, the Patricians. The Ventrue have ruled Kindred society longer than any other clan; they founded the Camarilla, they typically hold the Princedom in any Camarilla domain, they manage the long centuries of mortal banking, real estate, politics, finance. They are the establishment. They are old money — older than the money itself. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Dominate (mental command, the velvet glove of rulership), Fortitude, Presence. BANE: each Ventrue can only feed from a specific narrow type of mortal — a certain blood type, a certain profession, virgins, redheads, athletes, opera singers, soldiers in uniform, lawyers, surgeons. The pickier the Ventrue's restriction, the higher their status. Modern Ventrue rule the surviving Camarilla — and modern Ventrue are exhausted: the Beckoning has called many elders away, the Anarch defections gutted their political base, the Second Inquisition has destroyed centuries of careful infiltration. The clan that ran the world is now scrambling to keep its own territories. Ventrue Princes lean harder on Tremere rituals, harder on Lasombra muscle, harder on Banu Haqim enforcement. They still rule. They are still terrifying. But the foundation is cracking, and they know it. Ventrue characters are leaders, kingmakers, lawyers, judges — and they always, always have a plan.

16.content: Clan Banu Haqim — formerly the Assamites, the Children of Haqim, the Judges. An ancient clan from the Levant, the Banu Haqim were originally vampire-killers and judges: Kindred who killed other Kindred who broke ancient laws, enforcing balance through diablerie and execution. For centuries they were Independent, then Sabbat-aligned briefly, then Independent again. In V5 they made the historic move of joining the Camarilla — a controversial decision that gave the Tower desperately-needed enforcement and gave the Banu Haqim political legitimacy and protection in a world increasingly hostile to non-aligned clans. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Blood Sorcery (a different lineage from the Tremere's, focused on quietude and the hunt), Celerity, Obfuscate. BANE: they have a particular addiction — the blood of other Kindred. When they drink Vitae from another vampire, the bond is stronger, the temptation greater, the slide toward diablerie a constant pull. Modern Banu Haqim serve as Camarilla Sheriffs, executioners, judges of clan law, and the Tower's most feared enforcement specialists. They take their duty seriously. They were judges before they were warriors, and they remain judges still — even when the verdict is delivered with a blade. They do not banter. They keep their word. They will diablerize you if the law calls for it.

17.content: The Hecata are the unified clan of the dead — formed when the Giovanni, Cappadocians, Lamia, Samedi, and other death-touched bloodlines merged in the 21st century to survive. They specialize in necromancy (the Discipline of OBLIVION in V5, shared with Lasombra), wraith-binding, soul-trafficking, and the long art of holding territory without sect protection. The Giovanni were the dominant founders — a wealthy Italian crime family Embraced into the clan in the 15th century, who ruled through commerce, blood, and the dead they spoke with. Modern Hecata operate as a federated clan rather than a sect, with strong family ties and ruthless internal politics. They are independent — neither Camarilla nor Anarch — and have been running their own counter-offensive against the Second Inquisition through their long expertise in operating in the shadows of mortal society. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Auspex, Fortitude, Oblivion. BANE: their bite is excruciating to mortals (rather than the typical numbing/euphoric effect), making feeding messy, painful, and traumatic. The Hecata's central project (the Endless Night Project) seeks to break down the wall between living and dead. They are not friendly. They are not warm. They are old money, old grudges, old graves. A Hecata at the table brings their entire family with them — and the family does not forget slights, and the family includes the dead.

18.content: Clan Lasombra — the Magisters, the Shadow-Bound, the Keepers. A clan of patrician shadows, ruthless schemers, and Mediterranean nobility, the Lasombra spent five centuries as the political backbone of the Sabbat — and then, in the 21st century, executed one of the most consequential clan defections in modern Kindred history: they crossed to the Camarilla, abandoning the Sword of Caine for the Ivory Tower. Many old Sabbat Lasombra refused, were purged, fled, or remain bitter. The defection traded military power and Oblivion expertise for Camarilla legitimacy. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Dominate, Oblivion (a discipline of shadow and death, shared with Hecata), Potence. BANE: they cast no reflection in mirrors, they are not photographed by any device, they break electronic recordings within range. (In modern nights, this is both a blessing — surveillance fails on them — and a curse — explaining away security footage gaps requires constant effort.) Modern Lasombra in the Camarilla are still finding their place: many old Tower Kindred distrust them entirely, while the Lasombra distrust the Tower's softness. They bring a ruthless edge to Camarilla politics — and they bring the only widely-available counter to the Hecata's Oblivion expertise. They are not safe. They are useful.

19.content: The Ministry — formerly the Followers of Set, the Setites, the Tempters. Once a clan worshipping Set, the Egyptian god of darkness, the Ministry has rebranded itself in the modern nights into a more flexible religious-political organization that recruits across all faiths and traditions, united by the conviction that liberation comes through the breaking of false constraints — every law, every faith, every chain. Ministry Kindred are tempters, dealers, smugglers, blackmailers, prophets, drug-runners, freedom fighters, cult leaders. They believe every mortal and every Kindred has a chain of false belief or false attachment, and the Ministry's purpose is to find that chain and break it — for liberation, they say; for corruption, others say. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Obfuscate, Presence, Protean (their lineage of Protean is distinct from Gangrel — focused on serpentine forms). BANE: they have a sensitivity to bright light beyond standard sun-aversion — even strong artificial light pains and weakens them. The Ministry is the third major Independent clan alongside the Hecata and Banu Haqim factions, often working with both. Modern Ministry temples operate as cults, NGOs, drug rings, and sometimes legitimate religious organizations. They are charming, persuasive, and absolutely never to be trusted — but they will keep their bargains, because the breaking of bonds is sacred to them, and unkept bargains are themselves bonds.

20.content: Clan Tzimisce — the Dragons, the Old Country, the Fiends. A clan of monstrous Eastern European nobility, descended from boyars and warlords of the Carpathian Basin, the Tzimisce are flesh-shapers, soil-bound, and territorial to a degree no other clan matches. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Animalism, Dominate, and PROTEAN (their lineage focuses on flesh-shaping rather than animal forms — sometimes formerly called Vicissitude — allowing them to reshape themselves and others into anything they can imagine). BANE: they must sleep with two handfuls of soil from their birth-place (or a place of personal significance), or suffer increasing weakness and wasting each successive day. Tzimisce held vast Sabbat territories for centuries — they were the Sabbat's flesh-crafters, war-makers, monstrous anchor — and the Beckoning has called the bulk of the clan away to the Middle East alongside the Sabbat masses. What remains in V5 is sparse: a few elders in Eastern European holdings, scattered cells, ancient pacts, half-mythical figures. The Tzimisce are rarely encountered in standard chronicles, but when they appear, they bring centuries of grudge, expertise in violations no other clan permits itself, and the unsettling mastery of flesh that makes every encounter a horror. They are old country in the truest sense — a clan that remembers when the Carpathians were the edge of the world, and treats the modern era as an inconvenience to be outlasted.

21.content: Clan Ravnos — the Charlatans, the Wanderers, the Curse-Bearers. Once a clan of South Asian and Roma origin, masters of illusion and itinerant trade, the Ravnos suffered a near-extinction event in 1999 known as the WEEK OF NIGHTMARES: their Antediluvian Zapathasura awakened, the clan was driven to internecine slaughter in a global suicide-frenzy, and the surviving population was reduced to a tiny scattered remnant. Modern V5 Ravnos are rare, hunted, often broken — and the curse continues. CLAN DISCIPLINES: Animalism, Obfuscate, Presence, with a heavy focus on illusion. BANE in V5: they cannot rest in the same place two nights in a row — they are condemned to perpetual movement, finding new haven each daysleep. Ravnos in modern chronicles are wanderers, fortune-tellers, con artists, refugees from a clan that mostly does not exist anymore. They carry the trauma of the Week of Nightmares as a clan-wide PTSD, and many have made peace with their dwindling state by joining whatever gang or coterie will accept them. They are not numerous. They are not forgotten. They are the clan that survived its own apocalypse and now rides the night in small numbers, looking for whatever comes next.

22.content:
CAITIFF: the clanless. Embraced by Kindred whose own clan-blood was too thin or whose Embrace failed to imprint clan-curses, Caitiff have no Bane, no clan-disciplines (they can learn any common Discipline, with effort), and no clan-status. They are universally scorned by clan-bound Kindred who consider them bastards or accidents. The Anarchs accept them more readily than the Camarilla. Many Anarch Barons are Caitiff. Caitiff in modern chronicles often have stronger personal identities than clan-bound Kindred precisely because they cannot rely on inherited clan culture.

THIN-BLOODS (Duskborn): vampires of 14th, 15th, and even later generations — so distant from Caine that their blood barely functions as Vitae. They cannot Embrace successfully (their childer rise as further Thin-Bloods or fail entirely), their Disciplines are weak, but they have unique gifts no clan-blooded Kindred possesses: many Thin-Bloods can walk in dawn light briefly, eat mortal food, even father children (DHAMPIR — half-vampire offspring). Many practice THIN-BLOOD ALCHEMY — a discipline of mixing Vitae with mundane substances to produce single-use effects: temporary sun-resistance, brief bursts of clan Discipline, healing elixirs. The Camarilla mostly tries to kill Thin-Bloods on sight as harbingers of Gehenna. The Anarchs harbor and protect them. Thin-Blood populations have exploded in the 21st century — a sign of approaching apocalypse, or the next stage of the species, depending on who you ask.

23.content:
Disciplines are the supernatural powers granted by Kindred Vitae. Each clan has three (sometimes four) signature Disciplines they learn naturally; Kindred can learn other Disciplines by drinking the blood of one who possesses them, but the cost in Vitae and time is significant. MAJOR DISCIPLINES:

ANIMALISM — communication with and command of animals; calming or rousing the Beast in oneself or others.
AUSPEX — heightened perception, mind-reading, aura reading, prophetic visions, scrying.
CELERITY — supernatural speed; multiple actions per turn, blurred motion, dodging bullets.
DOMINATE — mental control; commands that mortals cannot resist, memory alteration, hypnotic suggestion.
FORTITUDE — supernatural resilience; resisting damage, including (at high levels) some sun-damage.
OBFUSCATE — concealment, invisibility, illusion, identity-shifting, mass perception manipulation.
POTENCE — supernatural strength; throwing cars, breaking steel, bone-shattering blows.
PRESENCE — supernatural charisma, fear, awe; commanding a room, terrifying enemies, seducing crowds.
PROTEAN — shapeshifting (Gangrel: animals; Ministry: serpents; Tzimisce: flesh); melding into earth.
BLOOD SORCERY — Tremere/Banu Haqim ritual magic; lasting effects bound by blood, scrying, curses.
OBLIVION — Lasombra/Hecata shadow and death magic; communing with wraiths, shadow manipulation, death-touch.
THIN-BLOOD ALCHEMY — Duskborn-only single-use elixirs.

Discipline use costs Vitae and raises Hunger. Powerful effects require Rouse Checks that risk Hunger gain. The more an Ancient elder uses their power, the more they must feed.

24.content:
BLOOD BOND (Vinculum): a Kindred or mortal who drinks from the same Kindred source three nights becomes mystically devoted to that source — they will defend their regnant, obey them, love them in a way that overrides reason. The bond runs from drinker to source. ONE DRINK: the drinker thinks fondly of the source. TWO DRINKS: the drinker is genuinely emotionally attached. THREE DRINKS: the bond is complete and effectively unbreakable except by Final Death of the regnant, distance over years, or rare ritual intervention. Kindred can be bonded to other Kindred (creating thralls) or to mortals (creating loyal servants). Bonds are weaponized constantly: Princes bond their Sheriffs, sires bond their childer, lovers bond each other. The Tremere can ritualize and accelerate bonds. Camarilla law prohibits forced bonding without Prince's permission — practice is more cynical.

GHOULS: mortals fed Kindred Vitae. A single drink grants supernatural strength, speed, resilience for roughly a month, with addiction following. Regular feeding maintains the ghoul indefinitely — they do not age, they gain limited Disciplines (typically one or two signature Disciplines of their domitor's clan at low levels), they live as long as the Vitae flows. Ghouls are servants, lovers, retainers, soldiers, day-shift bodyguards (they walk in sun while the domitor sleeps). A Kindred without ghouls is alone in the night. Ghouls without their domitor go mad with withdrawal — physical agony for days, then the slow return of mortal aging plus the trauma of unwanted memory.

25.content:
Kindred must feed on mortal blood to survive. Standard feeding: Kindred bites a mortal, drinks (typically in small amounts to avoid kill or detection), uses fangs to seal the wound. The mortal feels euphoria during the bite (most clans), often forgetting the experience or remembering it as a vivid dream. Most Kindred have established hunting patterns — clubs, hospitals, homeless shelters, willing Blood Dolls, established herds.

RESONANCE is the V5-era acknowledgment that mortal blood carries emotional flavor. A mortal in fear tastes CHOLERIC; in joy SANGUINE; in melancholy MELANCHOLIC; in calm PHLEGMATIC; in sexual passion or addiction, EMPTY. Each Resonance favors specific Disciplines: Choleric favors Celerity and Potence; Sanguine favors Blood Sorcery and Presence; Melancholic favors Fortitude and Obfuscate; Phlegmatic favors Auspex and Dominate. Skilled hunters cultivate herds with specific Resonances. The strongest mortals develop DYSCRASIAS — concentrated mystical effects that grant temporary Discipline boosts when consumed.

Feeding has consequences: mortal kill = Masquerade risk + Humanity loss; willing donor = preserved Humanity but logistical burden; addiction-feeding (drug users, alcoholics) = passes the addiction to the Kindred temporarily, with Hunger dice complications. The Hunt is a ritual; it is also nightly survival; it is also where most Kindred lose their humanity drop by drop. A Kindred who does not hunt soon does not survive.

26.content:
HUMANITY (V5) is a measure of how human the Kindred remains — how connected to the moral framework of mortal life. Humanity ranges 0-10 (most starting characters at 7). Higher Humanity = more empathy, fewer frenzies, more ability to pass for living (eat food at low levels, sleep at human hours, even sexual function with feeding). Lower Humanity = colder, more monstrous, more frenzy-prone, eventually sinking into WASSAIL (permanent loss of self to the Beast at Humanity 0).

Humanity is anchored by TOUCHSTONES: specific mortals (almost always living humans) who represent a value the Kindred has not yet abandoned. A Kindred has 1-3 Touchstones; each represents a CONVICTION (a personal moral rule the Kindred holds). Convictions might be: 'I will not kill children.' 'I will not feed on the unwilling.' 'I will protect my city.' 'Family comes first.' 'No one dies for my Hunger.'

When a Kindred violates a Conviction or witnesses a Touchstone harmed, they risk STAINS — marks against their Humanity that, if not cleared by remorse and processing in subsequent scenes, sink the Humanity score. Lose your Touchstone (death, betrayal, alienation) and you lose access to that Conviction. A Kindred at low Humanity who has lost all Touchstones is on the path to Wassail.

The system makes personal horror narratively measurable: every night, you decide what kind of monster you are willing to become. Most chronicles are stories about the slow erosion of Humanity through the choices necessary to survive.

27.content:
A Camarilla domain (or Anarch city, with structural variations) is governed by:

PRINCE: ultimate ruler. Holds the Right of Praxis (rule by force/political maneuver). Approves Embraces, judges Tradition violations, rules on territory disputes, negotiates with neighboring domains. Often Ventrue, sometimes Toreador or Tremere.

SENESCHAL: Prince's chief of staff. Steward of the city. Often the next-in-line should the Prince fall, or simply the most trusted advisor.

PRIMOGEN COUNCIL: clan elders advising the Prince. Each represented clan typically has one Primogen. The Primogen are the Prince's check — and sometimes the Prince's overthrow.

SHERIFF: capital police of the domain. Enforces Tradition violations, hunts fugitives, polices Masquerade. Often Banu Haqim, Brujah, or Gangrel when those are present.

HARPY: social arbiter. Tracks Status, manages reputation, controls who is in and out of fashion. Toreador most often. The Harpy can ruin a Kindred without ever raising a hand.

KEEPER OF ELYSIUM: maintains the neutral grounds (ELYSIUM — typically opera houses, museums, libraries) where violence is forbidden and intrigue takes formal shape. Often Toreador or Nosferatu.

SCOURGE: position created in modern nights to hunt unsanctioned Kindred (Caitiff, Thin-Bloods, illegal Embraces) within the domain. Not all cities have one; some abolish the position. Brutal work, often Banu Haqim.

WHIPS: Primogen's enforcers within their own clans, herding clanmates into political alignment.

The Anarch equivalent has BARONS (territorial leaders), Anarch Councils, and gang structure. Less formal, more democratic, more fractious. Anarchs often have roughly analogous functions (someone enforces, someone arbitrates) without the ranked titles.

28.content:
The JYHAD is the eternal war of the elders — a centuries-spanning game of chess in which Antediluvians, Methuselahs, and ancient elders manipulate younger Kindred as pieces. Most political conflicts in Kindred society are downstream of someone's Jyhad ploy. The further you investigate any Camarilla feud, Anarch alliance, or clan rivalry, the more you find the fingerprints of an elder you cannot reach.

ANTEDILUVIANS are the 13 clan founders, third generation, Embraced directly by the Second Generation (themselves children of Caine). Most are believed to be sleeping in TORPOR (vampire stasis), but most of the great cataclysms in Kindred history have been Antediluvians stirring — the Tzimisce woken in 1998, the Ravnos Antediluvian destroyed in 1999, the Lasombra and Tremere founders both consumed centuries ago in coups by their own clans. The few Antediluvians known to be active are world-shaping forces.

METHUSELAHS (3,000-10,000 years old) are scarcely less powerful — and the BECKONING has called most of them, alongside elders 1,000+ years old, to the Middle East. The world has been depopulated of ancient Kindred and the next generation is inheriting a fractured power structure. The Beckoning manifests as an irresistible psychic compulsion to travel east; few elders resist it for long.

GEHENNA is the apocalypse prophecy: the night the Antediluvians wake, devour their descendants, and reclaim the world. Camarilla orthodoxy denies it. Sabbat doctrine prepares for war against it. Anarchs mostly ignore it. The Hecata are studying the prophecies with active interest. Many believe Gehenna has already begun — the Beckoning, the Week of Nightmares, the Second Inquisition, the Thin-Blood explosion are all signs. The modern nights are the prelude.
